evals.name: New Launch - Full Guided Flow
evals.prompt: Rank my product B0GKR3D7SZ for "leakproof tumbler 40oz"
evals.expected_output: Full 9-phase campaign assessment for BruMate Era Flip 40oz tumbler (~$48, ~107 reviews). Should detect as NEW LAUNCH (honeymoon available), recommend Aggressive or Standard profile, generate 8-14 day ramp schedule, show cost breakdown with SFB proceeds, and wait for user confirmation before executing.
expectations: Output includes a Product Assessment Card with ASIN B0GKR3D7SZ
Output identifies the product category (Home & Kitchen, Tumblers, or similar)
Output includes a competition analysis for the target keyword
Output includes severity-labeled findings using [PASS], [WARNING], [CRITICAL], or [INFO]
Campaign type is identified as NEW LAUNCH or new launch with honeymoon phase mentioned
A funnel profile is selected and the SFB:ATC:PGV ratio is stated
A ramp schedule is generated with daily signal volumes across multiple day ranges
Cost breakdown shows gross LB spend and adjusted net cost after SFB proceeds
Output asks for user confirmation before executing the campaign
Output mentions the honeymoon phase or honeymoon window

evals.name: Re-ranking - Full Guided Flow
evals.prompt: My product B085DVHQ57 used to rank on page 1 for "insulated water bottle with straw" but dropped to page 4. Help me re-rank it.
evals.expected_output: Full 9-phase re-ranking campaign for Owala FreeSip 32oz (~$33, ~115K reviews). Should detect as RE-RANKING (no honeymoon), recommend Conservative profile, generate 60-90 day 3-phase ramp schedule (calibration, acceleration, stabilization), show higher total cost due to longer duration.
expectations: Output includes a Product Assessment Card with ASIN B085DVHQ57
Campaign type is identified as RE-RANKING or re-rank
Output mentions that no honeymoon phase is available for re-ranking
Strategy includes 3 phases: calibration (or signal calibration), acceleration, and stabilization
Campaign duration is 60-90 days or approximately 12 weeks
A Conservative or Standard funnel profile is selected
A ramp schedule shows graduated volume increases across the 3 phases
Cost breakdown accounts for the longer campaign duration
Output includes re-ranking specific checkpoints (day 14, 30, 56, or 84)
Output warns about not stopping the campaign abruptly (taper protocol)

evals.name: Quick-start Mode
evals.prompt: Quick campaign: B08CS95K8L, "nonstick frying pan 10 inch", 4 SFB, standard profile, 14 days
evals.expected_output: Skip Phases 1-4 (product assessment, competition, listing audit, campaign type). Should still run Phase 0a-0b (connection check + existing campaign check), then go straight to strategy confirmation with the user-specified parameters (4 SFB/day, Standard profile, 14 days), cost breakdown, and execution.
expectations: Output does NOT include a full Product Assessment Card (quick-start skips assessment)
Output uses 4 SFB per day as specified by the user
Output uses the Standard profile as specified
Campaign duration is 14 days as specified
ATC and PGV volumes are calculated from the Standard ratio (approximately 1:2:8-10)
Cost breakdown is shown with gross spend and adjusted net cost
Output asks for user confirmation before executing
The keyword "nonstick frying pan 10 inch" is used in the campaign setup

evals.name: Cost-only Exploration
evals.prompt: How much would a ranking campaign cost for B09XS7JWHH targeting "noise canceling headphones"?
evals.expected_output: Full assessment through cost analysis (Phases 0-6) for Sony WH-1000XM5 (~$250, ~19K reviews). Should detect as cost-only mode (no execution). Should select High-Price/Considered profile due to $250 price point. Should show detailed cost breakdown with SFB proceeds and ROI projection, but NOT proceed to execution.
expectations: Output includes product data for ASIN B09XS7JWHH
The High-Price or Considered Purchase funnel profile is selected (due to price >$50)
The SFB:ATC:PGV ratio reflects the high-price profile (approximately 1:2-3:10-15)
Cost breakdown shows gross LB spend, SFB sale proceeds, and adjusted net cost
ROI projection is included with estimated organic revenue
Output does NOT ask to execute or create projects (cost-only mode)
Competition level is assessed as HIGH or VERY HIGH for this keyword
Output mentions this is a high-consideration purchase category

evals.name: Budget-first Mode
evals.prompt: I have $300 to spend on ranking B004U3Y8OM for "vitamin d3 softgels". What can I do with that budget?
evals.expected_output: Budget-first analysis for Nature Made Vitamin D3 1000 IU (~$8.59, ~55K reviews). Should detect budget-first mode from the stated $300 budget. Should select Low-Price/Consumable profile (product under $15). Should present 2-3 campaign options that fit within $300, recommending focused single-keyword approach.
expectations: Output identifies the product as a low-price consumable
The Low-Price or Consumable funnel profile is selected (product under $15)
Output works backward from the $300 budget to determine campaign parameters
At least 2 campaign options are presented within the $300 budget
A recommendation is made (likely focused single-keyword for best results)
Daily SFB volumes are calculated to fit within the budget constraint
Cost breakdown shows how the $300 budget is allocated
Output notes that SFB proceeds partially offset the cost for this low-price product

evals.name: Non-US Region (UK)
evals.prompt: Rank my product B0CF2DJHFQ for "fabric shaver rechargeable" on amazon.co.uk
evals.expected_output: Full campaign flow for Philips 1000 Series Fabric Shaver on Amazon UK. Should detect non-US region and adjust to ATC+PGV only strategy (no SFB available). Should explain the SFB limitation clearly, show adjusted ratios, recommend higher volumes and longer duration, and show simplified cost structure without SFB proceeds.
expectations: Output detects the UK marketplace (amazon.co.uk)
Output clearly states that SFB is NOT available for UK region
Campaign uses ATC and PGV signals only (no SFB)
The ATC:PGV ratio is adjusted for non-US (no SFB component)
Output recommends higher signal volumes and/or longer duration compared to US equivalent
Cost breakdown omits SFB proceeds section (no purchases being made)
Output explains the limitation of non-US campaigns compared to US
The keyword "fabric shaver rechargeable" is used in the campaign
